relationships.name: hasComponent
relationships.definition: A missile or subsystem has a specific engineering component as a physical part
relationships.description: Structural composition relationship expressing physical containment or integration

relationships.name: partOf
relationships.definition: A component or subsystem is physically part of a larger assembly
relationships.description: Inverse of hasComponent; mereological inclusion

relationships.name: subsystemOf
relationships.definition: A functional system is a subsystem of a higher-level system
relationships.description: Functional hierarchy; subsystem fulfills specific functional role within parent

relationships.name: usesGuidance
relationships.definition: A missile employs a specific guidance mode or technology
relationships.description: Links missile instance to guidance taxonomy class

relationships.name: usesNavigation
relationships.definition: A missile uses a specific navigation technology during flight
relationships.description: Navigation technology used, possibly distinct from terminal seeker

relationships.name: usesPropulsion
relationships.definition: A missile is powered by a specific propulsion type
relationships.description: Propulsion system(s) used; may be multiple for booster-sustainer designs

relationships.name: usesWarhead
relationships.definition: A missile carries and delivers a specific warhead type
relationships.description: Warhead type employed; may be selectable for multi-role missiles

relationships.name: usesFuze
relationships.definition: A missile uses a specific fuze type for warhead initiation
relationships.description: Fuze determines detonation mode: proximity, impact, time, command

relationships.name: usesSensor
relationships.definition: A missile employs specific sensors for flight data, targeting or environment sensing
relationships.description: Sensor systems excluding the primary seeker; includes altimeter, IMU sensors, etc.

relationships.name: usesPowerSystem
relationships.definition: A missile uses a specific power generation or storage system
relationships.description: Electrical power source used during flight

relationships.name: usesCommunication
relationships.definition: A missile employs a specific communication system for datalink or command updates
relationships.description: Data link or communication technology enabling mid-flight updates or BDA

relationships.name: launchesFrom
relationships.definition: A missile can be launched from a specific platform type
relationships.description: Launch platform or vehicle from which the missile is deployed

relationships.name: launchedBy
relationships.definition: A missile is launched by a specific military unit, ship, aircraft or platform
relationships.description: Operational launcher; more specific than launchesFrom

relationships.name: targets
relationships.definition: A missile is designed to engage a specific target type
relationships.description: Intended target class; determines seeker and warhead selection

relationships.name: tracks
relationships.definition: A missile seeker actively tracks a specific target type
relationships.description: Seeker tracking capability related to physical phenomenon (IR signature, RCS, etc.)

relationships.name: homesOn
relationships.definition: A missile seeker homes on a specific physical phenomenon or signature
relationships.description: Physical homing phenomenon: IR emission, radar reflection, laser spot, etc.

relationships.name: guidedBy
relationships.definition: A missile is guided using a specific technology or method
relationships.description: Guidance technology active during any flight phase

relationships.name: controlledBy
relationships.definition: A missile is remotely controlled or updated by an external system during flight
relationships.description: External control authority during flight; applies to command-guided or datalink-updated missiles

relationships.name: manufacturedBy
relationships.definition: A missile is manufactured by a specific defense company
relationships.description: Primary production entity responsible for the missile system

relationships.name: developedBy
relationships.definition: A missile was designed and developed by a specific organization
relationships.description: R&D organization; may differ from manufacturer for government-developed systems

relationships.name: operatedBy
relationships.definition: A missile system is operated in service by a specific nation or military branch
relationships.description: Current or historical operational user of the missile system

relationships.name: exportedTo
relationships.definition: A missile has been exported to or licensed for production by another nation
relationships.description: Export transfer or licensed production agreements

relationships.name: variantOf
relationships.definition: A missile is a variant or derived configuration of a parent missile system
relationships.description: Variant relationship for missiles sharing a common design lineage with specific differences

relationships.name: derivedFrom
relationships.definition: A missile design is derived from or heavily based on another missile's design
relationships.description: Design lineage for missiles that evolved from an ancestor with significant modification

relationships.name: successorOf
relationships.definition: A missile is the successor in service to an earlier missile system
relationships.description: Generational succession in operational service

relationships.name: predecessorOf
relationships.definition: A missile was the predecessor in service to a later missile system
relationships.description: Inverse of successorOf; denotes which missile was replaced

relationships.name: counteredBy
relationships.definition: A missile can be countered or defeated by a specific countermeasure or system
relationships.description: Soft or hard kill countermeasure effectiveness relationship

relationships.name: jammedBy
relationships.definition: A missile guidance system is susceptible to jamming by a specific EW system
relationships.description: Electronic warfare effectiveness against missile sensor or guidance

relationships.name: interceptedBy
relationships.definition: A missile can be physically intercepted and destroyed by a specific interceptor
relationships.description: Hard kill defeat relationship; interceptor must achieve lethal kinematic solution

relationships.name: detectedBy
relationships.definition: A missile can be detected by a specific sensor or radar system
relationships.description: Detection relationship; influenced by RCS, IR signature and altitude of missile

relationships.name: trackedBy
relationships.definition: A missile is tracked in flight by a specific fire control or tracking radar
relationships.description: Continuous flight tracking by ground, ship or airborne sensor

relationships.name: poweredBy
relationships.definition: A missile is propelled by a specific propulsion type
relationships.description: Propulsion-level relationship; more general than usesPropulsion

relationships.name: communicatesWith
relationships.definition: A missile communicates with an external system during flight
relationships.description: Bidirectional or uplink communication during flight for updates or targeting

relationships.name: carriesPayload
relationships.definition: A missile carries a specific payload type beyond conventional unitary warhead
relationships.description: Non-standard payload: MIRV RVs, submunitions, EMP device, sensor, etc.

relationships.name: usesFuel
relationships.definition: A missile propulsion system uses a specific fuel or propellant chemistry
relationships.description: Propellant or fuel chemistry powering the missile

relationships.name: hasFlightPhase
relationships.definition: A missile undergoes a specific flight phase during its trajectory
relationships.description: Ordered sequence of flight phases; guidance and propulsion differ per phase

relationships.name: hasPerformance
relationships.definition: A missile has quantifiable performance parameters
relationships.description: Quantitative performance envelope: range, speed, CEP, altitude, G-limit

relationships.name: belongsToFamily
relationships.definition: A missile belongs to a broader missile family or program lineage
relationships.description: Family grouping for missiles sharing heritage, design philosophy or naming

relationships.name: compatibleWith
relationships.definition: A missile is mechanically, electrically or software-compatible with a specific platform or launcher
relationships.description: Integration compatibility for launchers, stations, rail systems, VLS cells

relationships.name: integratedWith
relationships.definition: A missile is fully integrated with a weapon system or fire control system
relationships.description: Full weapon system integration including software, IFF and targeting

relationships.name: defeatedBy
relationships.definition: A missile is defeated by a specific hard-kill or soft-kill system in combat or testing
relationships.description: Evidenced defeat in combat or qualification testing; stronger than counteredBy

relationships.name: supportsRole
relationships.definition: A missile supports a specific operational military role or mission
relationships.description: Operational role including deterrence, precision strike, air defense, etc.

relationships.name: deployedIn
relationships.definition: A missile has been operationally deployed or used in combat in a specific conflict or operation
relationships.description: Combat use history; provides validation of operational capability

relationships.name: qualifiedFor
relationships.definition: A missile has received certification or qualification for a specific platform or role
relationships.description: Formal qualification for integration; distinct from compatibility which may be potential

relationships.name: certifiedBy
relationships.definition: A missile has been certified by a specific national or international authority
relationships.description: Regulatory certification by military or civil authority

relationships.name: testFiredBy
relationships.definition: A missile has been test-fired by a specific organization, nation or program
relationships.description: Test firing history demonstrating range, guidance or warhead performance
